mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in real disposable income drives consumer spending, which boosts corporate earnings and lifts equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day SPX returns show no statistically significant correlation with RSAFS growth rates (Rank IC < 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.0%, borrowing costs compress corporate profit margins and reduce equity risk premiums, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0%, or the t-statistic on the rate coefficient in a regression of forward returns on FEDFUNDS is greater than -1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous weakness in industrial production and rising unemployment signals a demand-supply mismatch that erodes corporate pricing power, depressing equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO decline cross UNRATE increase) fails to predict negative SPX returns with a positive sign coefficient in a multivariate regression over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial output capacity reduces input cost inflation and supports margin expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: Weekly SPX forward returns exhibit zero or negative correlation with month-over-month INDPRO changes across all rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate above 5.5% triggers recession fears that compress equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward SPX returns remain positive or neutral in over 50% of instances where UNRATE breaches 5.5%, yielding a negative predictive accuracy below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising interest rates combined with contracting real disposable income force deleveraging and reduce consumption demand, sharply lowering equity valuations over a one-month period.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising FEDFUNDS and falling RSAFS fails to produce a negative Rank IC greater than 0.10 for SPX 20-day returns in a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth directly increases corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon through earnings revision cycles.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward SPX returns show no positive relationship with GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth rates, with a rolling correlation coefficient consistently below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.8 million units annually signal a credit crunch and weakening construction demand, which transmits to broader economic activity and depresses equity returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are not significantly lower (p > 0.05) when HOUST drops below 1800 compared to periods above this threshold, or the signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over a 2-year backtest.
